title: Configuring SYB for Unicode
breadcrumbs: Support for Unicode in the SYB Connector
text: Configuring SYB for Unicode To configure the Sybase connector to use Unicode, edit the application assignment file. To enable Unicode support in the Sybase connector: Specify the unicode connector option. To enable storage of the entire Unicode range (or SMP) in W packing code fields, also set the smp option. To enable Unicode support in the SQL workbench, sql ProcScript statement, and error messages, specify the keyword and charset utf 8 options. To enable support for service stored procedures, ensure that the $DEF_CHARSET is set to UTF8 . Example: Unicode Configuration [SETTINGS] $DEF_CHARSET = UTF8 [DRIVER_SETTINGS] usys$syb_params charset utf8 ,unicode, smp, keyword Copy Related Links Unicode Unicode is an industry standard designed to allow text and symbols from all languages to be consistently represented and manipulated by computers. unicode Enable or disable Unicode support in the MQL connector. keyword Adds double quotes around field and table names and sets the Sybase option quoted_identifier on. charset Set default character set used by the Sybase open client. $DEF_CHARSET Specify the default character set used for storing data in String fields with C packing code.
